베드로전서 2:1.content: Putting away therefore all wickedness, all deceit, hypocrisies, envies, and all evil speaking,

베드로전서 2:2.content: as newborn babies, long for the pure milk of the Word, that with it you may grow,

베드로전서 2:3.content: if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is gracious:

베드로전서 2:4.content: coming to him, a living stone, rejected indeed by men, but chosen by God, precious.

베드로전서 2:5.content: You also, as living stones, are built up as a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.

베드로전서 2:6.content: Because it is contained in Scripture, “Behold, I lay in Zion a chief cornerstone, chosen and precious: He who believes in him will not be disappointed.”

베드로전서 2:7.content: For you who believe therefore is the honor, but for those who are disobedient, “The stone which the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone,”

베드로전서 2:8.content: and, “a stumbling stone and a rock of offense.” For they stumble at the word, being disobedient, to which also they were appointed.

베드로전서 2:9.content: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God’s own possession, that you may proclaim the excellence of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.

베드로전서 2:10.content: In the past, you were not a people, but now are God’s people, who had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy.

베드로전서 2:11.content: Beloved, I beg you as foreigners and pilgrims, to abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul;

베드로전서 2:12.content: having good behavior among the nations, so in that of which they speak against you as evildoers, they may by your good works, which they see, glorify God in the day of visitation.

베드로전서 2:13.content: Therefore subject yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord’s sake: whether to the king, as supreme;

베드로전서 2:14.content: or to governors, as sent by him for vengeance on evildoers and for praise to those who do well.

베드로전서 2:15.content: For this is the will of God, that by well-doing you should put to silence the ignorance of foolish men:

베드로전서 2:16.content: as free, and not using your freedom for a cloak of wickedness, but as bondservants of God.

베드로전서 2:17.content: Honor all men. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king.

베드로전서 2:18.content: Servants, be in subjection to your masters with all respect: not only to the good and gentle, but also to the wicked.

베드로전서 2:19.content: For it is commendable if someone endures pain, suffering unjustly, because of conscience toward God.

베드로전서 2:20.content: For what glory is it if, when you sin, you patiently endure beating? But if, when you do well, you patiently endure suffering, this is commendable with God.

베드로전서 2:21.content: For you were called to this, because Christ also suffered for us, leaving you an example, that you should follow his steps,

베드로전서 2:22.content: who didn’t sin, “neither was deceit found in his mouth.”

베드로전서 2:23.content: When he was cursed, he didn’t curse back. When he suffered, he didn’t threaten, but committed himself to him who judges righteously.

베드로전서 2:24.content: He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we, having died to sins, might live to righteousness. You were healed by his wounds.

베드로전서 2:25.content: For you were going astray like sheep; but now you have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.
